reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. `[MetricSpace X]` matches "metric space"; `[ConnectedSpace X]` matches "connected" (and supplies nonemptiness, which is implicit in `ConnectedSpace` in Mathlib); `hX : ∃ x y : X, x ≠ y` matches "having more than one point" (i.e., at least two distinct points / nontrivial). The conclusion `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` coerces `univ` to its subtype and asserts that subtype is not countable; by `Set.countable_univ_iff` this is equivalent to `¬ Countable X`, i.e., X is uncountable as a type. No hypothesis is added or dropped, and the quantifier structure and typing are correct.
